name: Selecta UK
differentiator: Scale, brand partnerships (Starbucks, Coca-Cola), Foodies micro-market concept
opportunity: Slow to serve small/medium sites. Corporate SLAs mean inflexible service. Premium pricing leaves room for competitive indie pricing.
notes: £137M turnover 2023. 3 London depots. Acquired The Vending People. Europe's largest vending operator.

name: The Vending People
opportunity: Now part of Selecta — potential integration friction. May deprioritise smaller accounts.
notes: Acquired by Selecta. Fleet figures overlap with Selecta's.

name: The Jar Healthy Vending
differentiator: Healthy/premium niche, curated product range, brand identity
opportunity: Narrow niche — only healthy/premium. Leaves conventional snack/drink market open. Likely premium pricing limits to affluent areas.
notes: Strong brand positioning in wellness space. Good benchmark for healthy vending differentiation.

name: Vending Services Ltd
opportunity: Kent-based — less responsive to central/north London. Established but may lack modern tech (cashless, telemetry).

name: Westways Vending
opportunity: Reading-based — south London fringe only. No central/north/east London presence. Aging business model possible.
notes: Long-established family business. Primarily M4 corridor and Home Counties.

name: MorVend
opportunity: Legacy operator — may be slower to adopt cashless/telemetry tech. Potential acquisition target or partnership.
notes: Family-run since 1968. One of London's longest-running independents.

name: Nu Vending
differentiator: Strong London focus, micro market capability
opportunity: Direct competitor in London. Differentiating on service speed, niche locations, or healthier options could carve space.
notes: London's foremost vending solutions provider. Also offers micro markets — a growing segment.

name: The Snack Choice
opportunity: No London presence. Benchmark comparator — similar stage/scale to target operation.
notes: Romell/Sade's operation. Useful as peer benchmark for growth trajectory and operational model.

name: Compass Group (Canteen)
differentiator: Scale, bundled FM contracts, global brand
opportunity: Only serves large corporates with full FM contracts. SMEs and independents are not their market. Vending is add-on, not core focus.
notes: World's largest food service company. Vending is a small part of their offering.

name: Aramark
opportunity: Same as Compass — locked into large FM contracts. No interest in small sites. Vending not core.
notes: US-headquartered FM giant. UK presence via corporate contracts.

name: Sodexo
differentiator: Global scale, bundled FM, strong public sector presence
opportunity: Same pattern — large FM contracts only. Strong in public sector which is hard to crack anyway.
notes: French FM giant. UK operations significant but vending is minor component.

name: YumMy Vending
differentiator: Customisable product selection, multilingual support, fresh food options
opportunity: Small operator — likely limited fleet and coverage areas. Competing on service quality and responsiveness.
notes: London-based independent. Offers snacks, drinks, and fresh food including baguettes and samosas.

name: Cafepoint
service_model: Fully managed — machines, micro markets, coffee solutions
target_sectors: Offices, finance, law firms, hospitality, warehouses
differentiator: Micro market specialist, all operations London-based, premium workplace focus
opportunity: Premium/corporate focus may overlook smaller offices and blue-collar sites. Micro market model requires larger spaces — not suitable for all locations.
notes: UK's leading independent micro market provider. Phone: 020 8523 3000. Strong in City/Canary Wharf corporate space. Entire operation based in London.

name: Dupont Latour
service_model: Design, build and install micro markets and food service spaces
differentiator: Micro market design/build specialist, modular solutions, bespoke installations
opportunity: More of a fit-out company than an operator. Could be a partner rather than competitor — they build the space, you operate it.
notes: Established 2011. Designs food service counters, cafes, restaurant interiors, and micro market installations. More fit-out than operations.
